info.name: Declut API
info.description:
Declut — trust-first escrow marketplace API for buying/selling pre-owned household items. NestJS + MongoDB. Payment is held in escrow until a buyer-held confirmation code is entered by the seller at the meetup.

AUTH PATTERN: every protected route expects `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`. Run Auth → Register or Auth → Login first — both auto-save `accessToken`/`refreshToken` into collection variables via a test script, so every other request in this collection picks them up automatically. When the access token expires (15m default), run Auth → Refresh Token.

ADMIN: admin-only requests (the Admin folder) use a separate `{{adminAccessToken}}` variable so you can stay logged in as a regular user and an admin at the same time. Bootstrap the first admin with `npm run seed:admin -- <email> <password> [name]` (see CLAUDE.md), log in with Auth → Login using those credentials, and copy the returned accessToken into the `adminAccessToken` collection variable.

Set the `baseUrl` collection variable to wherever the API is running (defaults to http://localhost:3000).

request.description: Basic liveness + Mongo connectivity check. No auth required.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Google sign-in (google-auth-library, no Firebase) + email/password, both converging on backend-issued JWT access/refresh tokens.

request.description: Creates an email/password account. Always role: user — admin accounts are never created through this endpoint (see scripts/seed-admin.ts). Rate-limited. Auto-saves accessToken/refreshToken to collection variables.
Generic "invalid credentials" on either a missing user or wrong password. Rate-limited. Auto-saves accessToken/refreshToken.

idToken is verified server-side against Google's public keys via google-auth-library (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID as audience) — Firebase is never involved. Rejects (403) if the resolved email belongs to an admin account; admins must use email/password. Rate-limited. Auto-saves accessToken/refreshToken.
Rotates both tokens — the presented refresh token is revoked and a new pair issued. A revoked/expired/reused token is rejected. Rate-limited. Auto-saves the new accessToken/refreshToken.
Revokes the presented refresh token's session row. Does not affect other logged-in devices.

item.name: Users
item.description: Profile, KYC status, cached trust score and rating stats.

request.description: Full private profile — email, kycStatus, trustScore, bank details, etc. Only ever returned to the user themselves.
All fields optional. Bank details are required before a seller can accept a checkout (used to create a Paystack subaccount).
Public-safe subset only: name, verified (boolean, from kycStatus), trustScore, avgRating, reviewCount. No email, no bank details.

item.name: Listings
item.description: Create/edit/archive/delete, radius + keyword search. All routes require login (no guest browsing in v1).

request.description: Backend never touches image bytes — returns a short-lived signed payload the mobile app uses to upload directly to Cloudinary, then sends the resulting URLs back in Create Listing's images field.

All query params optional. Supplying lat/lng runs a $geoNear radius search (default radius DEFAULT_SEARCH_RADIUS_KM); keyword alone ranks by text relevance; neither param falls back to newest-first. Remove query params you don't need.
category: furniture|electronics|appliances|kitchenware|clothing|decor|books|other. condition: new|like_new|good|fair|poor. location is {lat, lng}, not GeoJSON — the service converts it internally.
Owner-only (403 otherwise). All fields from Create Listing are optional here.
Owner-only. Removes it from search without deleting it.
Owner-only. Soft delete (status: deleted) — never a real Mongo delete, since a Transaction may reference this listing.

item.name: Favorites
item.description: Buyer save/unsave listings, list saved listings.

request.description: Idempotent upsert — favoriting an already-favorited listing is a no-op, not an error.

item.name: KYC
item.description: NIN + facial liveness, gated at first listing (sellers) / first purchase (buyers) — not mandatory at signup. Admins are permanently exempt.

request.description: NIN + facial liveness via QoreID (behind a KycProvider interface — swappable vendor). The selfie image is held in memory only long enough to forward to the provider; never persisted. Sets kycStatus to pending, then verified/rejected (or reverts to unverified on a provider/config error, not a false rejection). Rate-limited (5/min) given the payload size and cost per call.
Audit trail only — pass/fail + reference ID per attempt, never images.

item.name: Offers
item.description: Buyer offers below asking price; seller accepts/rejects/counters. An accepted offer seeds a Transaction at its amount instead of list price.

request.description: Offers where you're the buyer or the seller.
Only visible to the buyer/seller on that offer.
Can't offer on your own listing. One pending offer per (buyer, listing) at a time (409 on a second). Auto-expires after OFFER_EXPIRY_DAYS if untouched.
Only the party who did NOT propose the current amount can accept/reject/counter — it must be "their turn". An accepted offer's amount is what Create Transaction uses instead of list price.
Terminal-status's the current offer (status: countered) and creates a new offer in the chain (parentOffer points back), with proposedBy flipped and a fresh expiry.
Only the current proposer can withdraw, and only while pending.

item.name: Transactions & Payments
item.description: Paystack checkout → escrow → buyer-held confirmation code → seller-triggered release, 10% commission at release. See the webhook note before trying to call it manually.

request.description: Initializes a Paystack transaction (held via subaccount split, manual settlement) and returns paystackAuthorizationUrl for the mobile app to open. offerId is optional — omit it to buy outright at list price, or pass an accepted offer's ID to check out at the negotiated amount. One pending checkout per (buyer, listing) at a time. Rate-limited.
Transactions where you're the buyer or seller. confirmationCode is only ever included for the buyer, and only while status is escrow_active/awaiting_inspection.
Only visible to the buyer/seller on that transaction.

Seller-only. Correct code triggers a Paystack payout to the seller minus commission and marks the transaction completed. Wrong code increments a counter; hitting MAX_CODE_ATTEMPTS auto-flags the transaction disputed for admin review.
Buyer-only, and only while still pending_payment — once paid, resolution requires an admin action (release/refund), never a direct cancel.

NOT for manual testing — Paystack calls this directly, authenticated by HMAC-SHA512 signature (not a bearer token) over the raw request body. Included here for completeness/documentation only. Idempotent: replaying a webhook for an already-processed transaction is a no-op.

item.name: Reviews
item.description: Buyer and seller rate each other after a transaction completes. Feeds User.avgRating/reviewCount and the trust score formula.

request.description: Only once the transaction is completed, only by the buyer or seller on it, one review per reviewer per transaction (409 on a repeat). role/reviewee are derived server-side from the transaction, never trusted from the body.
Reviews received by that user (as reviewee) — open to any logged-in user, since a public rating history is the point.
Both reviews on a deal (if they exist) — only visible to the buyer/seller on that transaction.

item.name: Notifications
item.description: FCM push only (no chat/websockets). Firebase touches nothing outside this module. A push failing never breaks the underlying operation (payment released, offer accepted, etc.).

request.description: platform is optional (ios|android). Upserts by token — re-registering the same device (even under a different account) moves it to the current caller.
Scoped to the caller's own tokens — deleting a token you don't own is a silent no-op, not an error.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Requires JwtAuthGuard + AdminGuard (being logged in is not being an admin). Use the "Admin login" request in Auth with a bootstrapped admin account (npm run seed:admin), then set the adminAccessToken collection variable manually or via a small test script.

request.description: Ongoing admin-creation path once at least one admin exists. The FIRST admin can't come from here (chicken-and-egg) — see scripts/seed-admin.ts. The promoted user's existing JWT still carries the old role claim until they log in again.
status: unverified|pending|verified|rejected. Setting verified also triggers a trust score recalculation (the same +20 flat bonus KycService.verify() applies automatically on a real provider pass).
Unlike the public search, includes archived and deleted listings.
confirmationCode is always stripped, even for admins — "only ever returned to the buyer" holds with no exception.
Only valid on stalled/disputed transactions (400 otherwise). Triggers the same Paystack payout path as a normal code confirmation, minus commission, then marks completed.
Only valid on stalled/disputed transactions (400 otherwise). reason is optional, stored on the audit log entry.
Removes an abusive/violating review and recalculates the affected user's avgRating/reviewCount and trust score.
